meta.title: Gallery Carousel
meta.description: A full-width gallery section with a dark card container, content header, and a single-image carousel with navigation and pagination controls

parentTsx: { useBlockProps, InspectorControls, useInnerBlocksProps, store as blockEditorStore } from '@wordpress/block-editor'

requiredPatterns.usage: Empty state handling for content and media areas
Sync block name with heading content in list view
Swiper initialization and cleanup in editor
Render child image blocks inside swiper-wrapper
Load image data from ID in child block
Drag-and-drop image upload in child block editor
Sidebar image picker with focal point in child block
Disable loop/autoplay when block or children focused
Pass carousel settings to frontend JS
Custom appender for adding new carousel items

qualityChecklist: Parent uses providesContext to share settings with children
Parent uses renderAppender: false with custom Appender
Parent uses shouldDisplay() for hasInnerBlocks check
Parent defines Swiper config inline (not in a helper function or as getCarouselConfig - match existing pattern)
Parent uses useSelect with hasSelectedInnerBlock to detect child focus
Parent passes isEditing (block OR children focused) to disable loop/autoplay
Parent uses useRefEffect for Swiper initialization with cleanup
Parent passes carousel settings via data attributes in PHP
Child has parent restriction in block.json
Child uses shouldDisplay() for empty states
Child image has responsive aspect ratio classes
Frontend JS defines Swiper config inline
Frontend JS reads settings from data attributes
Frontend JS initializes Swiper on DOMContentLoaded
TSX cn() and PHP class_name() have identical conditional logic
Arrow SVGs are reusable from CardsCarousel or custom

parent.name: eyebrow
parent.description: Small uppercase label above the heading

parent.name: description
parent.description: Body text below heading or in right column on desktop

parent.name: buttons
parent.description: Array of button objects (secondary variation in dark context)

child.name: image
child.description: Gallery image with focal point support

section.notes: Outer section wrapper. py-6 mobile (24px), sm:py-16 desktop (64px). The 'gallery-carousel' class is for frontend JS targeting.

outerContainer.notes: Standard theme container with responsive max-width and side gutters

card.classes: dark relative bg-charcoal rounded-3xl overflow-hidden px-[18px] py-9 sm:px-8 sm:py-24 flex flex-col gap-8 sm:gap-16
card.notes: The dark charcoal card container. rounded-3xl = 24px (Figma 25px, acceptable 1px diff). Contains noise texture overlay as first child. gap-8 (32px) mobile, gap-16 (64px) desktop matches Figma.

noiseTextureOverlay.childImageClasses: absolute inset-0 w-full h-full object-cover mix-blend-color-dodge opacity-[0.08]
noiseTextureOverlay.notes: Decorative noise texture overlay on the card. The noise texture image must be a tileable PNG. Uses mix-blend-mode: color-dodge at 8% opacity as shown in Figma. The rounded-3xl and overflow-hidden ensure it clips to the card shape.

headerSection.notes: Uses ThemeHeading with columns=2 for the two-column desktop layout (eyebrow+heading left, description+button right). On mobile (columns=2 still uses md:grid-cols-2 breakpoint), stacks naturally. The heading maps to text-header-2 (Anton font).

carouselWrapper.notes: Grid-based swiper parent that contains controls row and swiper element

controlsRow.notes: Controls row BETWEEN header and carousel per spec. Contains progress bar and nav buttons. gap-4 (16px) between pagination and nav. mb-6 (24px) gap below controls before carousel image.

paginationProgressBar.classes: swiper-pagination flex-1 relative! h-1 bg-charcoal/20 dark:bg-white/20 rounded-full overflow-hidden
paginationProgressBar.notes: Swiper progressbar pagination. Uses theme gradient system for fill (defined in swiper.css). The bg-white/20 matches Figma's rgba(255,255,255,0.5) approximately (theme uses 20% opacity for consistency with existing CardsCarousel pattern).

navigationContainer.notes: Contains prev/next arrow buttons. gap-2 (8px) mobile, gap-4 (16px) desktop. Figma shows 16px gap at both sizes but mobile may need tighter.

navButtonPrev.notes: Uses existing carousel-nav-btn CSS class from swiper.css. 40x40 mobile, 48x48 desktop. Border, rounded-full, gradient hover animation.

navButtonNext.notes: Uses existing carousel-nav-btn CSS class. Same dimensions as prev.

swiperContainer.notes: The Swiper container element. Uses unique class 'gallery-carousel-swiper' for frontend JS targeting (distinct from 'cards-carousel-swiper'). ref={swiperRef} in TSX.

swiperWrapper.notes: Standard Swiper wrapper containing slide elements. Uses useInnerBlocksProps in TSX.

childSlide.notes: Each gallery carousel item is a swiper slide wrapping a single image.

childImageContainer.notes: Container for the image with rounded corners. The image itself handles aspect ratio.

childImage.element: img (via ImageDropUploader in TSX, wp_get_attachment_image in PHP)
childImage.classes: w-full! h-auto! object-cover aspect-[1.08] sm:aspect-[2.45] rounded-2xl
childImage.notes: Responsive aspect ratio: ~1.08:1 on mobile (318x294 from Figma), ~2.45:1 on desktop (1312x536). Object-cover ensures fill. Rounded-2xl (16px) for smooth corners on the image.

swiperConfig.notes: Single image per view at all breakpoints (no column breakpoints needed). Uses getCarouselConfig helper pattern from CardsCarousel. spaceBetween: 0 since slides are full-width. No breakpointsBase needed since slidesPerView is always 1.

mobile.cardGap: gap-8 (32px) between header and carousel sections
mobile.headerLayout: Stacked single column (ThemeHeading columns=2 but below md breakpoint stacks)

tablet.notes: Inferred - transitions between mobile and desktop. Card padding switches to desktop values at sm. ThemeHeading still stacked until md.

emptyState.parent: Show ThemeHeading area when selected (shouldDisplay). Show carousel wrapper when hasInnerBlocks or selected. Show Appender when selected or no inner blocks.
emptyState.child: Show ImageDropUploader placeholder when selected and no image (shouldDisplay).

optionalFields.notes: Heading is technically optional too but ThemeHeading handles this. Image in child block shows placeholder via ImageDropUploader.

editorBehavior.blockNaming: renameBlock() syncs heading content to block name in list view
editorBehavior.swiperInEditor: Uses useRefEffect to initialize Swiper in editor. Disables loop/autoplay when isEditing (block or any child selected). Re-initializes when showNavigation, showPagination, loopCarousel, or isEditing changes.
editorBehavior.childAppender: Custom Appender component shown below carousel when block is selected or has no inner blocks

carousel.notes: Swiper A11y module handles aria-live announcements and keyboard navigation

images.notes: Images use wp_get_attachment_image which includes alt text from media library

noiseTexture.description: The Figma design shows a noise texture overlay on the dark card with mix-blend-mode: color-dodge and 8% opacity. This creates a subtle grainy texture on the charcoal background.
noiseTexture.implementation: Add a noise texture PNG image (tileable, grayscale noise pattern) to blocks/GalleryCarousel/resources/NoiseTexture.png. Render it as an absolutely-positioned img inside the card div, before all content.
noiseTexture.cssClasses: absolute inset-0 w-full h-full object-cover mix-blend-color-dodge opacity-[0.08] pointer-events-none rounded-3xl
noiseTexture.generationNotes: The developer should either: (1) extract the noise texture from Figma manually, (2) generate a tileable noise pattern PNG (e.g., 200x200px grayscale noise), or (3) use a CSS-based noise pattern with SVG filter. Option 3 is recommended for performance: use an inline SVG feTurbulence filter.

arrowSvgs.description: The navigation arrow SVGs can be copied from the existing CardsCarousel block at blocks/CardsCarousel/resources/ArrowLeft.svg and ArrowRight.svg.

decisions.question: Where should pagination/navigation controls be positioned?
decisions.decision: Controls go BETWEEN the header and carousel on ALL breakpoints (above the image)
decisions.rationale: Spec explicitly states: 'Put the pagination and navigation between the heading and the carousel in both the mobile and desktop versions.' This overrides the Figma desktop layout where controls appear below the image. Mobile Figma already shows controls above image. Per conflictResolution.specVsFigma: 'spec'.

decisions.question: Should the noise texture overlay be implemented?
decisions.decision: Yes, include noise texture overlay on the dark card background
decisions.rationale: Both desktop and mobile Figma designs clearly show a noise texture with mix-blend-color-dodge at 8% opacity on the card. This is a new pattern not in existing blocks. CSS SVG feTurbulence filter is recommended for performance over a PNG.

decisions.question: Should this block use the dark mode toggle pattern (pseudo-element) or direct card styling?
decisions.decision: Use direct dark card styling - always dark, no toggle
decisions.rationale: The Figma shows a single dark variation with no light alternative. The design uses a direct bg-charcoal card with rounded corners, noise texture, and contained padding. This is different from the CardsCarousel dark-mode toggle pattern. The block is always dark by design, similar to FullWidthMedia's approach but with the card wrapper.

decisions.question: What heading size token to use for the 56px Anton heading?
decisions.rationale: Figma shows 56px Anton at desktop. Theme text-header-2 = 48px base -> 60px at 48rem (sm). 60px is the closest to 56px (4px difference, acceptable). This matches the FullWidthMedia and CardsCarousel blocks which also use headingSize={2}.

decisions.question: What aspect ratios should the gallery images use?
decisions.decision: aspect-[1.08] on mobile (default), aspect-[2.45] on sm+ breakpoint
decisions.rationale: Desktop Figma: 1312x536px = 2.449:1 ratio (~2.45). Mobile Figma: 318x294px = 1.081:1 ratio (~1.08). These are exact measurements from the Figma design context output.

decisions.question: What section and card padding values to use?
decisions.decision: Section: py-6 sm:py-16. Card: px-[18px] py-9 sm:px-8 sm:py-24
decisions.rationale: Desktop Figma: outer py-64px (py-16), card px-32px py-96px (px-8 py-24). Mobile Figma: outer py-24px (py-6), card px-18px py-36px (px-[18px] py-9). These match the FullWidthMedia pattern for section padding (py-6 sm:py-16) and use card-specific values.

decisions.question: What content does the child inner block contain?
decisions.decision: Image only - no text, no buttons, no title
decisions.rationale: Spec states: 'The gallery should allow only images. Each image will be an inner block.' The Figma confirms this showing only images in the carousel with no overlaid text or buttons.

decisions.question: How many slides per view?
decisions.rationale: Both desktop and mobile Figma show a single full-width image per view. No multi-column carousel. No 'columns' attribute needed (unlike CardsCarousel which has 1-3 columns).

decisions.question: Which button variation should the ThemeHeading buttons use?
decisions.decision: btn-secondary variation (outline button with white border in dark context)
decisions.rationale: Figma button shows: bg-charcoal, border-white, rounded-full, p-16px, text-white uppercase. This matches btn-secondary in dark mode: outline style with border-white, text-white, and gradient sweep on hover.

decisions.question: What gap between header sections and carousel controls?
decisions.decision: gap-8 (32px) mobile, gap-16 (64px) desktop between major sections within the card
decisions.rationale: Desktop Figma card uses gap-64px between header and carousel sections. Mobile uses gap-32px. These map to gap-16 and gap-8 respectively.

decisions.question: What border-radius for the card?
decisions.rationale: Figma shows 25px border-radius. Tailwind rounded-3xl = 24px. 1px difference is acceptable per token mapping rules.

decisions.question: What border-radius for the carousel images?
decisions.rationale: The Figma image container appears to have rounded corners. Following the FullWidthMedia pattern which uses rounded-xl for inner media. However, looking at the Figma more carefully, the image corners are subtly rounded. rounded-2xl (16px) provides a harmonious relationship with the rounded-3xl (24px) card.

decisions.question: Should ThemeHeading use columns=2 or columns=1?
decisions.rationale: Desktop Figma shows a two-column layout: eyebrow+heading on the left, description+button on the right, with gap-64px between columns. This matches ThemeHeading columns=2 which applies md:grid-cols-2 gap-x-20.

decisions.question: Should the progress bar background be white/20 or white/50?
decisions.rationale: Figma shows rgba(255,255,255,0.5) but existing CardsCarousel uses dark:bg-white/20 for the progress bar background. Per conflictResolution.patternDeviation: 'convention', follow existing pattern for consistency across carousel blocks.

notes: Test responsive aspect ratios: images should be nearly square on mobile (~1.08:1) and wide landscape on desktop (~2.45:1)
Verify noise texture is visible as subtle grain on the dark card background
Verify controls (pagination + navigation) appear between header and carousel at all viewport sizes
Verify progress bar gradient matches theme gradient system (neon-green -> blue)
Verify navigation arrow hover states show gradient sweep animation
Verify single image case hides controls appropriately (swiper-controls CSS handles this)
Verify ThemeHeading two-column layout at desktop, stacked at mobile
Test with images of various aspect ratios to ensure object-cover works properly

frontendJs.description: Create GalleryCarousel.js following the CardsCarousel.js pattern but with simplified config (slidesPerView always 1, no column breakpoints).
frontendJs.notes: Must use a UNIQUE selector class different from 'cards-carousel-swiper' to avoid conflicts.

noiseTextureApproach.cssApproach: Use an inline SVG with feTurbulence for a performant, resolution-independent noise pattern. Apply as a pseudo-element or a hidden SVG referenced via CSS url().
noiseTextureApproach.fallback: If CSS noise is too complex, use a small tileable PNG (200x200px, grayscale noise, exported from Figma or generated).

arrowSvgReuse.description: Copy ArrowLeft.svg and ArrowRight.svg from blocks/CardsCarousel/resources/ to blocks/GalleryCarousel/resources/. These are the same arrow icons used in the existing carousel navigation.
arrowSvgReuse.notes: If the arrows in the Figma design differ, extract from Figma instead. Based on visual inspection, the arrows appear identical to CardsCarousel.
